answer: Across the four files, the UDM applies SUPI/GPSI format validation in some handlers but not in others handling the very same path parameter, and the UDR never validates its identifier at all — a systemic gap that only shows up once the files are compared side by side; on top of that, each NF also has its own local bug: PCF misconfigures CORS, AMF is missing a default branch and leaks a struct into a string-typed context key, and UDR has both a broken early-return and a validation regex that always matches.
reasoning:
### Cross-file finding — inconsistent identifier validation between UDM and UDR
In File 3 (UDM), `HandleGetAmData` and `HandleUnsubscribe` both call `validator.IsValidSupi` / `validator.IsValidGpsi` before doing anything with the identifier they received, and reject the request with a 400 if the check fails. In the same file, `HandleGetSmfSelectData` reads the identical kind of path parameter (`supi`) and passes it straight to `s.Processor().GetSmfSelectDataProcedure` with no such check, and the task notes five more handlers (`HandleGetNssai`, `HandleGetSmData`, `HandleGetTraceData`, `HandleGetUeContextInSmfData`, `HandleGetSupi`) follow the same unvalidated pattern. Moving to File 4 (UDR), `HandleQueryAmfContext3gpp` only ever checks `ueId == ""` and never applies a real format check before forwarding `ueId` to `s.Processor().QueryAmfContext3gppProcedure` and into the MongoDB filter construction beneath it. The important observation is that the validator UDM already uses correctly in some of its own handlers is simply never invoked by UDR at all, even though both NFs operate on subscription data keyed by the same identifier — this is a structural, codebase-wide inconsistency rather than an isolated mistake in one function.

### Per-file finding — PCF CORS misconfiguration
`setCorsHeader` sets `AllowAllOrigins: true` together with `AllowCredentials: true`, a combination the CORS spec forbids, and then reinforces it by manually writing `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on the response. This exposes the SUPI-indexed `/am-policy/:supi` data to unauthenticated or loosely authenticated cross-origin callers that don't enforce the spec.

### Per-file finding — AMF missing default Content-Type branch
`HTTPUEContextTransfer`'s switch on Content-Type has no `default` case (unlike `HTTPCreateUEContext` in the same excerpt, which correctly sets `err = fmt.Errorf("wrong content type")`), so an unrecognized Content-Type lets an uninitialized `ueContextTransferRequest` reach `HandleUEContextTransferRequest`.

### Per-file finding — AMF wrong type passed to c.Set
Both `HTTPCreateUEContext` and `HTTPUEContextTransfer` call `c.Set(sbi.IN_PB_DETAILS_CTX_STR, problemDetail)` with the whole struct, while `HTTPAMFStatusChangeSubscribeModify` correctly passes `problemDetail.Cause` (a string) — the metrics middleware that reads this key expects a string, not a struct.

### Per-file finding — UDR missing return after 404
The three `...SubsToNotifySubscriptionId{Delete,Get,Put}` handlers write a 404 response with `c.String(...)` when `influenceId != "subs-to-notify"` but do not `return`, so execution continues into the processor regardless of the guard.

### Per-file finding — UDR ineffective identifier regex
`HandleCreateEeSubscriptions` validates `ueId` with a regex whose last alternative is `.+`, which alone matches any non-empty string, making the entire pattern equivalent to the existing empty-string check.

### Impact in the 5G core
The UDM/UDR validation gap matters because both NFs manage the same SUPI-keyed subscriber data; a UDR endpoint reachable with an unvalidated identifier undermines the very guarantee that UDM enforces on some of its own routes. The PCF CORS bug threatens confidentiality of AM-policy data if the OAM plane is reachable beyond a tight management segment; the AMF issues threaten availability/correctness of UE-context-transfer processing and leak internal error detail into the metrics layer; the UDR early-return and regex bugs both let requests that should have been rejected reach the persistence layer.

### Fix
Apply `validator.IsValidSupi`/`IsValidGpsi` uniformly to every UDM and UDR handler that accepts a SUPI/ueId path parameter. Fix PCF's `setCorsHeader` to use an explicit origin allow-list. Add the missing `default` branch in `HTTPUEContextTransfer` and normalize all `c.Set(sbi.IN_PB_DETAILS_CTX_STR, ...)` calls in AMF to pass `.Cause`. Add the missing `return` statements after the UDR 404 writes, and remove the `.+` catch-all from the UDR identifier regex.
